audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition with Psalm 40/Jeremiah/Habakkuk citation texture; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for shadow, once-for-all offering, access, mutual encouragement, severe warning, endurance.

verses.restored: For Law, having shadow of coming good things, not the very image of the matters, by the same sacrifices they offer continually year by year, is never able to complete those drawing near.
Otherwise would they not have ceased being offered, because those serving, once cleansed, would no longer have consciousness of sins/misalignments?
But in these there is reminder of sins/misalignments year by year.
For it is impossible for blood of bulls and goats to take away sins/misalignments.
Therefore, entering into the world, he says: Sacrifice and offering you did not desire, but body you prepared for me.
In whole-burnt offerings and offerings concerning sin you did not delight.
Then I said: Look, I have come—in scroll of book it has been written concerning me—to do your will, O Divine.
Saying above, Sacrifices and offerings and whole-burnt offerings and offerings concerning sin you did not desire nor delight in, which are offered according to Law,
then he said: Look, I have come to do your will. He takes away the first to establish the second.
In that will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Messiah once for all.
And every priest stands daily serving and offering often the same sacrifices, which are never able to take away sins/misalignments,
but this one, having offered one sacrifice concerning sins/misalignments into the continuous, sat down at right hand of the Divine,
from then waiting until his enemies be placed as footstool of his feet.
For by one offering he has completed into the continuous those being sanctified.
And the holy Spirit also witnesses to us; for after saying,
This is the covenant that I will covenant with them after those days, says Lord: giving my laws upon their hearts, and upon their mind I will write them,
and their sins/misalignments and their lawlessnesses I will certainly remember no more.
Now where release/forgiveness of these is, there is no longer offering concerning sin/misalignment.
Therefore, siblings, having boldness for entrance of the holy places in the blood/life of Jesus,
which he inaugurated for us as new and living way through the veil, that is, his flesh,
and having great priest over the house of the Divine,
let us draw near with true heart in full assurance of trust, hearts sprinkled from harmful conscience and body washed with clean water.
Let us hold fast the confession of hope without wavering, for the One promising is trustworthy.
And let us consider one another for stirring up love and beautiful/good works,
not abandoning our gathering together, as custom for some, but encouraging, and so much more as you see the day drawing near.
For if we willingly sin/misalign after receiving knowledge of truth, sacrifice concerning sins/misalignments no longer remains,
but a fearful expectation of judgment and zeal of fire about to consume the opposing ones.
Someone setting aside Law of Moses dies without mercies on testimony of two or three witnesses.

How much worse punishment do you think will be deserved by the one trampling the Son of the Divine, considering common the blood/life of covenant in which one was sanctified, and insulting the Spirit of grace?
For we know the One saying: Vengeance is mine; I will repay. And again: Lord will judge his people.
Fearful it is to fall into hands of living Divine.
But remember the former days in which, having been enlightened, you endured great contest of sufferings,
sometimes being publicly exposed to reproaches and pressures, sometimes becoming partners with those so treated.
For you had compassion on prisoners and accepted seizure of your possessions with joy, knowing yourselves to have a better and remaining possession.
Therefore do not throw away your boldness, which has great reward.
For you have need of endurance, so that having done will of Divine you may receive the promise.
For yet a very little while, the coming one will come and will not delay.
But my righteous/just one will live by trust; and if one draws back, my soul/life has no pleasure in that one.
But we are not of drawing back into ruin, but of trust into preserving of soul/life.

translation_notes: Psalm 40's body prepared language follows Greek textual tradition; Hebrew/Greek difference should be flagged in second pass.
Warning language is severe but ends with confidence: we are of trust into preserving life.
Gathering together is for mutual stirring of love and good works, not institutional control.
safety_note: Hebrews 10 warning passages must not be used to terrorize sensitive consciences or coerce attendance. The pastoral movement is access, confidence, mutual encouragement, endurance, and preservation of life.
